headlines.description: Tottenham went top of the Premier League table with a 1-0 win at Luton Town on Saturday, despite playing the whole of the second half with 10 men.
headlines.shortLinkText: 10-man Spurs go top of table after win at Luton

headlines.description: Chelsea secured back-to-back Premier League victories for the first time since March after coming from behind to thrash Burnley 4-1 on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Sterling key as Chelsea secure consecutive wins

headlines.description: A resurgent Everton beat Bournemouth 3-0 on Saturday to claim their first home victory of the season and leave the visitors in the relegation zone.
headlines.shortLinkText: Everton beat Bournemouth for first home win

headlines.description: Fulham secured a comfortable 3-1 win over Sheffield United at Craven Cottage on Saturday to leave the visitors bottom of the Premier League table, in a game overshadowed by a horrible injury to United's Chris Basham.
headlines.shortLinkText: Fulham beat Sheffield United 3-1 after horror injury to Basham

headlines.description: Substitute Scott McTominay struck twice deep into stoppage time to snatch a 2-1 win for Manchester United over Brentford in a thrilling end to their Premier League clash at Old Trafford on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Late McTominay double helps Utd beat Brentford

headlines.description: Nottingham Forest's winless run stretched to four Premier League games after they were held to a 0-0 draw at Crystal Palace on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Forest and Palace play out nervous stalemate
